ingredients.name: Monument at Takabocchi Kogen Quasi-National Park
ingredients.localName: 高ボッチ高原国定公園の碑
ingredients.entityContext: Takabocchi Kogen is featured in Season 2 when Rin Shima camps alone at the highland plateau and enjoys a stunning sea of clouds view overlooking Lake Suwa. The monument and plateau scenery are faithfully recreated in multiple episodes.

ingredients.name: Makaino Farm
ingredients.entityContext: Makaino Farm appears as a stop during the girls' group camping trip around the Fujinomiya area, where they enjoy the pastoral scenery near the base of Mt. Fuji.

ingredients.name: Old build. Shimobe ES & JHS (Motosu HS)
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒409-2936 Yamanashi, Minamikoma District, Minobu, Tokiwa, 450
ingredients.entityContext: This abandoned school building served as the real-world model for Motosu High School, the school that Nadeshiko, Chiaki, and Aoi attend and where the Outclub (Wild Camping Activities Club) holds its meetings. It is one of the most iconic pilgrimage sites from the anime.

ingredients.name: Seifuken Restaurant
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒409-2936 Yamanashi, Minamikoma District, Minobu, Tokiwa, 726
ingredients.entityContext: Seifuken appears as a local diner in Shimobe visited by characters during one of their Minobu-area camping excursions, representing the everyday rural eateries the show celebrates.

ingredients.name: Near the Cosmo Nambu SS
ingredients.localName: コスモ石油 / (有)稲葉石油 南部SS付近
ingredients.address: 9172-63 Nanbu, Nambu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2212, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The area near this gas station in Nambu appears during Rin's solo motorcycle camping journey through the Minamikoma region in Season 2, capturing the quiet roadside scenery typical of her solo rides.

ingredients.name: Koan Camping Ground
ingredients.address: 2926 Nakanokura, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Koan Camping Ground on the shore of Lake Motosu is the very first campsite shown in the series. Rin camps here alone in Episode 1 while Nadeshiko stumbles in lost and they share a view of Mt. Fuji together, marking the beginning of their friendship.

ingredients.name: Free Parking Lot Fuji View
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒403-0005 Yamanashi, Fujiyoshida, Kamiyoshida, 字剣丸尾5597-84
ingredients.entityContext: This free parking area near Lake Motosu offers the same iconic Mt. Fuji framed-by-trees view recreated in Yuru Camp's opening visuals and early episode establishing shots.

ingredients.name: Hottarakashi Onsen
ingredients.entityContext: Hottarakashi Onsen, famous for its panoramic Mt. Fuji view from the open-air bath, is visited by Nadeshiko and Chiaki during the group's Koshu Valley camping trip in Season 2, where they soak while watching the sunrise.

ingredients.name: Motosuko Observation Park
ingredients.address: 2926 Nakanokura, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Motosuko Observation Park overlooks Lake Motosu with a classic Mt. Fuji backdrop. This view is one of the most reproduced in the anime and appears on the 1000-yen bill, visited by characters throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Nanbu Bridge
ingredients.description: Nanbu Bridge (南部橋) — local bridge in Minamikoma, Yamanashi, near Utsubuna Park and 南部氏館跡. Not listed on Google Maps, but the coordinates fall in this area — a verified scene location. Navigate by coordinates / Street View.
ingredients.entityContext: Nanbu Bridge is a background location along Rin's motorcycle route through the Minamikoma district during her solo winter camping trips depicted in Season 1.

ingredients.name: Kanayama Terrace
ingredients.address: 10階, 4 Chome-6-25 Kanayama, Naka Ward, Nagoya, Aichi 460-0022, Japan

ingredients.name: Korobokkuru Hutte
ingredients.localName: ころぼっくるひゅって
ingredients.address: 霧ヶ峰車山肩（無番地, Suwa, Nagano 392-0008, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Korobokkuru Hutte is the charming alpine cafe at Kirigamine/Kurumayama plateau visited in Season 2, where Rin stops during her solo ride and enjoys hot cocoa with a sweeping highland view, making it one of the most-cited pilgrimage cafes from the show.

ingredients.name: Entrance Square
ingredients.address: Hamana, Imabetsu, Higashitsugaru District, Aomori 030-1501, Japan

ingredients.name: Koan Main Building Central Lodge
ingredients.localName: 浩庵本館セントラルロッジ
ingredients.address: 2926 Nakanokura, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3104, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Central Lodge at Koan Camping Ground is the reception and common building adjacent to the lakeside campsite where Rin stays in Episode 1, shown when she checks in and warms up after a cold ride.

ingredients.name: Hoshakuzan Kozenji Temple
ingredients.entityContext: Kozenji Temple in Komagane, Nagano, is visited during the Ina Valley / Komagane sightseeing segment in Season 2, with its famous cedar-lined approach faithfully depicted in the episode.

ingredients.name: Lawson Minami Alps Kaido Shop
ingredients.localName: ローソン 南アルプス街道店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア
ingredients.entityContext: This Lawson convenience store along Route 52 appears in Yuru Camp as one of the roadside stops Rin makes during her solo motorcycle journeys through the South Alps Highway corridor.

ingredients.name: Shibireko Suimeisou
ingredients.address: 3378 Yamaho, Ichikawamisato, Nishiyatsushiro District, Yamanashi 409-3602, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Shibire Lake and its lakeside inn Suimeisou appear in Season 2 as the destination for a solo camping episode, featuring the tranquil forested lake atmosphere that the show is known for.

ingredients.name: A-Co-op Farmers Pier Midori Store
ingredients.localName: A・コープファーマーズ ピアみどり店
ingredients.entityContext: This supermarket near Kurumayama/Kirigamine appears in Yuru Camp as the grocery stop Rin uses before her highland camping trip to stock up on ingredients, reflecting the show's emphasis on camp cooking preparation.

ingredients.name: Tsuetsuki Pass Observation Cafe Kaze no Uta
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
ingredients.entityContext: The observation cafe at Tsuetsuki Pass near Kirigamine features in Season 2 as a scenic stop during Rin's drive through the Suwa highlands, with sweeping views of the Japan Alps.

ingredients.name: Takabocchi Mountain
ingredients.entityContext: Takabocchi Mountain's summit area is a key Season 2 location where Rin camps solo and wakes to a dramatic sea of clouds stretching over the Suwa Basin at sunrise, widely considered one of the most beautiful moments in the series.

ingredients.name: Panoramic Observation Deck of the Alps
ingredients.localName: アルプス連峰パノラマ展望台
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒399-0711 Nagano, Shiojiri, Kataoka, 高ボッチ高原キャンプエリア（テント・タープエリア
ingredients.entityContext: This observation deck at Takabocchi Kogen offers the panoramic Alps view depicted when Rin gazes toward the Southern Alps during her solo highland camp in Season 2.

ingredients.name: New Laundry Life Minobu
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒409-2412 Yamanashi, Minamikoma District, Minobu, Tsunouchi, 県道 10 号線 3052
ingredients.entityContext: This coin laundry in Minobu town appears in Yuru Camp during Nadeshiko's bike camping trip in Season 2, where she stops to do laundry mid-journey, a charming slice-of-life detail the show depicts with affection.

ingredients.name: Hayataro Onsen Komakusa-no-yu Hot Spring
ingredients.localName: 早太郎温泉 こまくさの湯
ingredients.entityContext: Komakusa-no-yu is the hot spring bath visited by the group in Komagane during their Ina Valley camping trip in Season 2, shown as a relaxing post-hike soak with mountain views.

ingredients.name: Mount Fuji YMCA Global Eco Village
ingredients.localName: 富士山YMCA グローバル・エコ・ヴィレッジ
ingredients.entityContext: The Mt. Fuji YMCA Eco Village near Makaino Farm appears as a campsite in the Fujinomiya area group trip, representing one of the campgrounds in the shadow of Mt. Fuji visited by the Outclub.

ingredients.name: Near the Tire Garden Nambu
ingredients.localName: タイヤガーデン 南部店付近
ingredients.address: 9172-33 Nanbu, Nambu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2212, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The roadside area near this shop in Nambu appears along Rin's motorcycle route through the Minamikoma valley, captured as background scenery during her solo winter ride.

ingredients.name: Nezu Bridge
ingredients.address: 1 Chome-28-9 Nezu, Bunkyo City, Tokyo 113-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Ice cream factory and direct sales store
ingredients.localName: 道の駅 朝霧高原 アイス工房
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒418-0101 Shizuoka, Fujinomiya, Nebara, 宝山 492-14
ingredients.entityContext: The Asagiri Kogen roadside ice cream shop at Michi-no-Eki Asagiri Kogen is visited by Nadeshiko and others during their Fujinomiya camping trip, one of several local food stops the anime uses to showcase the region's dairy culture.

ingredients.name: Minobu-Manju Bench
ingredients.address: Tsunouchi, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2412, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Minobu Manju Bench in Minobu town is a fun local landmark visited by Nadeshiko during her solo bike camping trip in Season 2, where she samples the local manjuu sweet, a signature food moment from that arc.

ingredients.name: Jinbagatayama Campground
ingredients.address: 1636 Okusa, Nakagawa, Kamiina District, Nagano 399-3801, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Jinbagatayama Campground in the Ina Valley offers a spectacular elevated view of the Ina basin, and is visited in Season 2 as part of the group's Nagano camping adventure, with the sweeping vista faithfully reproduced in the anime.

ingredients.name: Yamanashishi Station
ingredients.entityContext: Yamanashi City Station appears as a transit point in the Koshu Valley episodes, used by the group when they travel by train during their multi-day camping trip in Season 2.

ingredients.name: Fumotoppara Campground
ingredients.localName: ふもとっぱら(キャンプサイト)
ingredients.entityContext: Fumotoppara Campground at the base of Mt. Fuji is featured in Season 1 as the destination for the Outclub's first big group camping trip, offering a wide open field with Mt. Fuji towering directly overhead, one of the most visually striking settings in the series.

ingredients.name: No.8: Ozaki Ryuo's stone monument
ingredients.localName: 第八番 尾崎龍王の石碑
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒409-3602 Yamanashi, Nishiyatsushiro District, Ichikawamisato, Yamaho
ingredients.entityContext: This historical stone monument along the Fuji River valley route is passed by Nadeshiko during her solo bicycle camping journey in Season 2, one of several cultural waypoints shown along her route through Minamikoma.

ingredients.name: Eishodo
ingredients.address: 3024 Tsunouchi, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2412, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Eishodo is the confectionery shop in Minobu where Nadeshiko buys Minobu manju during her solo bike trip in Season 2, prominently featured as she excitedly tastes the local specialty.

ingredients.name: Shinanoya
ingredients.address: Ginza Peers Bldg., １ 階, 8 Chome-6-22 Ginza, Chuo City, Tokyo 104-0061, Japan

ingredients.name: New Japan's Top Three Night Views Observation Plaza
ingredients.localName: 新日本三大夜景 展望広場
ingredients.entityContext: This Yamanashi night-view observation point near Hottarakashi is referenced in Season 2 as one of the scenic stops during the group's Koshu Valley trip, showcasing the sparkling nighttime panorama over the Kofu Basin.

ingredients.name: Yashajin Hutte
ingredients.entityContext: Yashajin Hutte at the Yashajin Pass trailhead in Minami-Alps City appears in Season 2 as a rest stop during a mountain approach, with its rustic highland atmosphere fitting the show's appreciation for remote mountain scenery.

ingredients.name: Yamada Farm Fruit Farm
ingredients.localName: 山田農園フルーツファーム
ingredients.entityContext: Yamada Farm near Yamanashi City is visited during the Koshu Valley episode arc in Season 2, where the group enjoys the fruit-growing landscape characteristic of the Kofu Basin wine country.

ingredients.name: Marumo Machinery Yatsugatake Store
ingredients.localName: マルモ機械 八ヶ岳店

ingredients.name: Kirigamine Venus
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒392-0008 Nagano, Suwa, Shiga, 霧ケ峰７７１８－９
ingredients.entityContext: Kirigamine Venus Line is the scenic highland road driven by Rin during her Kirigamine/Kurumayama camping trip in Season 2, with the open moorland views depicted in multiple beautiful driving shots.

ingredients.name: Ashiyasu Dam Selected Civil Engineering Heritage Observation Deck
ingredients.localName: 芦安堰堤 選奨土木遺産 展望台
ingredients.entityContext: The Ashiyasu Dam observation deck in Minami-Alps City appears briefly during Rin's motorcycle journey through the Hayakawa valley region, reflecting the anime's habit of including engineering and infrastructure landmarks along its routes.

ingredients.name: Takashima Castle
ingredients.entityContext: Takashima Castle on the shores of Lake Suwa appears in Season 2 when Rin explores the Suwa area, with the castle's picturesque lakeside setting shown during her sightseeing before the Takabocchi camping segment.

ingredients.name: Nakanokura Tunnel
ingredients.entityContext: The Nakanokura Tunnel on Route 300 near Lake Motosu is passed by Rin on her motorcycle during her Lake Motosu camping trip, a recognizable landmark on the approach to Koan Camping Ground.

ingredients.name: Near the Unnamed Observatory
ingredients.localName: 名前のない展望台付近
ingredients.entityContext: This area near an unnamed Yamanashi viewpoint appears as a roadside scenic stop along one of Rin's solo riding routes, capturing the rolling highland scenery east of the Kofu Basin.

ingredients.name: Utsubuna Station
ingredients.address: Utsubuna, Nambu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-2305, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Utsubuna Station on the Minobu Line is the station Nadeshiko uses to start her solo bicycle camping journey in Season 2, shown as she arrives by train and excitedly sets off on her bike into the Minamikoma countryside.

ingredients.name: Selva Minobu
ingredients.address: 2309-200 Iitomi, Minobu, Minamikoma District, Yamanashi 409-3423, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Selva Minobu is a supermarket in Minobu town where Nadeshiko shops for supplies during her solo bike camping trip in Season 2, one of the mundane but affectionately depicted stops along her route.

ingredients.name: Kurumayamakata Parking Lot
ingredients.entityContext: The Kurumayama plateau parking lot near Kirigamine is where Rin parks her scooter before setting up her solo camp in Season 2, with the expansive highland grassland view and Mt. Fuji visible in the distance depicted in multiple scenic shots.
